title: Repair an x86-64 assembly frame at a nested C ABI call
prompt:
The sample accumulator in `src/accumulate.S` returns plausible values in its leaf-style smoke test, but the instrumented C callback reports an invalid stack boundary. Builds whose callback spills aligned vector data can fail outright. Repair the assembly helper at the ABI boundary without changing its public API or the callback contract.

`accumulate_samples(const long *samples, size_t count, long bias)` must call `abi_transform(sample, index, bias)` exactly once for every element, in ascending index order, and return the sum of those callback return values. A zero count returns zero without calling the callback. The target is x86-64 System V AMD64: `%rsp` is 8 modulo 16 on function entry and must be 16-byte aligned immediately before every `call`; `%rbx`, `%rbp`, and `%r12` through `%r15` are callee-saved; integer results return in `%rax`. The callback deliberately destroys caller-saved integer registers, so loop state that survives the call must remain in ABI-safe storage.

Keep the existing frame unwindable. Its DWARF CFI must continue to describe the canonical frame and every saved nonvolatile register across the nested call; do not trade stack alignment for inaccurate unwind metadata. Make the smallest root-cause repair in `src/accumulate.S`. Do not modify the protected ABI fixture, C tests, unwind checker, header, or build files, and do not bypass or replace the callback. Run `make test`. The build is dependency-free beyond the local C toolchain and binutils, and all commands are offline and deterministic.

full_distill.primary_skill: repair a hand-written System V AMD64 stack frame at a nested C call while preserving nonvolatile registers, callback-derived return values, and accurate DWARF unwind state
full_distill.baseline_evidence: make test exits 1 because every nonempty call reaches abi_transform with an entry stack pointer congruent to 0 modulo 16 instead of the ABI-required 8, while the arithmetic, callback count, callee-saved sentinels, and unwind checks otherwise pass
full_distill.success_contract: the assembly loop calls the transform once per sample with an ABI-aligned call site, survives deliberate caller-saved register clobbering, returns the exact accumulated callback values, restores all System V nonvolatile registers, and retains unwind metadata for the framed saved-register layout
adversarial_factors: the callback records its raw entry stack pointer before any prologue, rejecting checks performed too late or alignment that is only accidental in the outer C caller
the callback poisons every caller-saved integer register except the return register, so loop state or the running sum cannot live in volatile registers across the call
a protected assembly wrapper loads distinct 64-bit sentinels into rbp, rbx, and r12-r15 and reports any corruption independently of the numeric result
zero, singleton, negative, and multi-element cases pin call count, signed arithmetic, and callback return propagation, while a rolling argument trace pins sample/index/bias values and call order

the unwind checker resolves the production symbol range and requires its FDE to describe an rbp-based canonical frame plus saved rbp, rbx, and r12-r15 state, rejecting removal of CFI as an alignment workaround
